summary: Read-only deep map of every subsystem the Phase-2 review-job shell must integrate, to ground the Phase-2 plan
logs: Deep-reading 8 Phase-2 subsystems (read-only) to ground the plan

maps.subsystem: Review pipeline orchestrator — `orchestrate()` and the Temporal workflow body that wraps it
maps.currentShape:
The deterministic 9-stage spine lives in `apps/backend/src/review/pipeline/orchestrator.ts::orchestrate(ctx: ReviewPipelineContext): Promise<ReviewPipelineResult>` (export at line 351). It is a 1:1 TS port of the frozen Python `orchestrate_review_pipeline`, with the per-chunk `ReviewContextV1` build (`buildChunkContext`, line 929) fused in.

INPUT — ONE typed object `ReviewPipelineContext` (line 205): `{ repo: ReviewPipelineRepoCtx, pr: ReviewPipelinePrCtx, activities: ReviewActivityPorts, limits: ReviewPipelineLimits, state: ReviewWorkflowState }` plus OPTIONAL seams the workflow body injects: `linkedIssues?`, `suggestedReviewers?`, `logger?: StageLogger`, `claimCheck?: () => Promise<void>`, `onPlaceholderTeardown?: () => Promise<void>`, `arbitrationNow?: string`, `enrichment?`, `manifestSnapshots?`, `platformExposedLabels?`. `repo` carries `repoUrl`, `changedPaths`, and a pre-allocated `workspaceHandle: WorkspaceHandle`. `pr` carries prMeta, githubInstallationId, headSha, runId, reviewId, repositoryId, policyRevision, prNumber, changedLineRanges, parentFindings, parentReviewId. All UUIDs are wire STRINGS.

STAGE SPINE (Step numbers in code): claim-check→clone (line 368, OUTSIDE try) → 1a loadRepoConfig → 1b computePolicyRules (fail-open via stageOutcome) → claim-check → 2 classify (+classifier-failure-ratio>0.1 degradation) → 2a filterReviewPaths → 2a.1 EARLY-EXIT: if path_filters emptied a non-empty review set, post an ADVISORY review (aggregate one notice → walkthrough → runPostStage) and `return` skipping chunk/fanout (lines 471-525) → 3 [chunkAndRedact || staticAnalysis] parallel → 4 selectCarryForwardWithFallback (fail-open 'review every chunk') → M-A5 CHUNK CAP `MAX_CHUNKS_PER_REVIEW=100` on `selection.to_review` (line 571) → 5 fanOutReview (concurrency=`limits.chunkConcurrency`; per-chunk buildChunkContext+reviewChunk via stageOutcome raiseAfterLog) → 6 dedupFindings → claim-check → 7 aggregate → M-A3 `MAX_INLINE_FINDINGS=250` cap → maybeAppendConfigNotice → 7.2 applyPolicyPostFilter (inline, pure) → 7.5 citationValidate (optional port) → 7.6 persistReviewFindings (fail-open) → 7.7 applyArbitrationStep (GATED on `ports.applyArbitration !== undefined && sa !== null`) → 8 generateWalkthrough → 8a persistReviewWalkthrough (fail-open) → FINAL claim-check (FIX #10) → 9 runPostStage (postReview || postCheckRun) → `finally` Step 10 cleanup (releaseWorkspace by workspace_id). The fail-open model is `degradation.ts::stageOutcome(stage, opts, async()=>...)` — swallow+record_stage(error)+append `<stage>_failed` by default; `raiseAfterLog` re-raises; CancelledFailure ALWAYS re-raised; `skipOutcome()` suppresses success emit.

The workflow body `apps/backend/src/workflows/review_pull_request.workflow.ts::reviewPullRequest(rawPayload)` does everything AROUND orchestrate: parse payload → GATE `startReviewForWebhook` (tenancy recheck + PR-mutex acquire; non-accepted short-circuits) → extract `mutexId` → build `claimStillHeld`/`abortIfClaimLost` (renewal-backed lease) → OUTER try (BF-5/BF-13 run-transition) wrapping INNER try/finally (non-cancellable mutex+workspace release) → placeholder post → enrich PR files → allocate workspace → ANALYSIS_STARTED → fetch linked issues/suggested reviewers → manifest fetch/parse → load parent findings → build ctx (injecting claimCheck=abortIfClaimLost, onPlaceholderTeardown, arbitrationNow=`workflowInfo().startTime`) → `orchestrate(ctx)` → `runLifecycleBookkeeping` (flip findings to delivery outcome from `state.postedReview`) → ANALYZED → finalizeReviewRun → finally releases mutex+workspace in `CancellationScope.nonCancellable`.

Activities reach orchestrate through `ReviewActivityPorts` (activity_ports.ts:96), wired by `workflows/activity_proxy.ts::makeActivityPorts()` — 18+ per-activity `proxyActivities()` stubs mapping COMPACT port names (clone, reviewChunk, aggregate, cleanup) to REGISTERED worker names (cloneRepoIntoWorkspace, bedrockReviewChunk, aggregateFindings, releaseWorkspace) with per-activity RETRY_POLICIES.
maps.whatPhase2Changes:
The Phase-2 review-job shell is the `JobHandler` (`(job: ReviewJobV1, signal: AbortSignal) => Promise<void>`) that `runOneJob` invokes. It must do IN-PROCESS, with NO Temporal, everything `reviewPullRequest` does around orchestrate():

1. RESOLVE the payload — `core.review_jobs` carries run_id/review_id/installation_id/delivery_id only; the shell must load the full `ReviewPullRequestPayloadV1` (it is NOT on the job row). Plan must name the source (the outbox row / a review_requests table) — currently unverified.

2. REPLACE the Temporal seam — build a NON-Temporal `ReviewActivityPorts` that calls the real activity FUNCTIONS directly (e.g. `postReviewResults(input)`, `bedrockReviewChunk`/`doReview`, `aggregateFindings`) instead of `proxyActivities()` stubs. This replaces `makeActivityPorts()` entirely; RETRY_POLICIES (Temporal at-least-once retry curves) must be re-expressed as in-process retry/no-retry wrappers or dropped, because the whole job re-runs from scratch on crash (ADR-0077: re-run the whole review, no per-activity replay).

3. RE-EXPRESS the around-orchestrate lifecycle WITHOUT Temporal primitives: gate/placeholder/allocate/enrich/ANALYSIS_STARTED/issue-reviewer-fetch/orchestrate/bookkeeping/ANALYZED/finalize, plus the mutex+workspace release that today lives in `CancellationScope.nonCancellable` — the shell must run that in a plain finally honoring the abort signal. `arbitrationNow` (today `workflowInfo().startTime`) and run-id must come from the job, not the SDK.

4. WIRE the abort signal — `runOneJob` passes `work.signal` (AbortController) to the handler on lease-loss OR runtime-ceiling. The shell must thread an abort-aware `claimCheck` (gate #1+#4: ONE liveness clock) and ensure NO external write happens after abort.

5. WIRE the LLM ledger — construct the review LlmClient WITH `LlmInvocationLedger.fromDsn(dsn)` so paid Bedrock calls dedupe across re-runs (gate #2). Today the ledger is OPTIONAL and the plan must prove it is injected in the shell's client.

6. DECOUPLE from `@temporalio/workflow`/`@temporalio/common` — orchestrator.ts, degradation.ts, posting.ts all import these directly (metricMeter, inWorkflowContext, CancelledFailure, ApplicationFailure, ActivityFailure). The shell runs in plain Node, so these must resolve to no-ops/equivalents or the modules must be refactored.

hazards.title: orchestrate() + degradation.ts + posting.ts have HARD compile-time + runtime coupling to @temporalio/workflow and @temporalio/common
hazards.detail: degradation.ts:30-31 imports `CancelledFailure` and `metricMeter, inWorkflowContext` from @temporalio/*; posting.ts:24-27 imports `ActivityFailure, ApplicationFailure`; the orchestrator transitively depends on both via stageOutcome and runPostStage. `metricMeter`/`inWorkflowContext` are workflow-sandbox APIs that THROW or no-op outside a workflow execution. The 'is orchestrate() pure/injectable enough to call outside Temporal?' answer is NO as-is: the data SHAPE is injectable (one typed ctx, all ports optional/injected), but the degradation + posting leaves reach into Temporal sandbox globals. Phase 2 must verify these resolve cleanly in plain Node (inWorkflowContext() likely returns false → metric emits no-op, which is acceptable, but CancelledFailure/ApplicationFailure detection in posting.ts's extractDroppedStateFromPostFailure depends on the SAME error classes the in-process post activity raises — that holds since post_review_results.activity.ts also imports ApplicationFailure from @temporalio/common).

hazards.title: Gate #3 (post idempotency) is ALREADY solved by core.posted_reviews 2-phase atomic claim — but ONLY if the shell preserves stable run_id/review_id across re-runs
hazards.detail: post_review_results.activity.ts::doPost (842) is fully idempotent across re-runs: Phase-1 `INSERT INTO core.posted_reviews (pr_id, marker) ON CONFLICT (pr_id) DO NOTHING RETURNING pr_id` + an embedded HTML marker (markerFor, 108) + the IFF CHECK (degraded↔review_id NULL) + a lost-claim path that dispatches idempotent `updateReview` and INHERITS the persisted publication_outcome. A second re-run LOSES the claim and refreshes the body, never double-posting. BUT the AD-4 stale-write guard (`assertCurrentRun`, line 951) RAISES StaleWriteError if the run_id is not the current run — so the shell MUST replay the SAME run_id on re-run (or the guard rejects the re-run as superseded). ADR-0077 says re-run from scratch; if the shell mints a NEW run_id per attempt, the stale-write guard + the lost-claim window interact badly. Plan must pin run_id semantics (per-job, stable across attempts) — `core.review_jobs.run_id` is stable per job row, which is the right anchor.

hazards.title: Gate #2 (LLM ledger) idempotency key is keyed on chunk_id whose cross-re-run stability is UNVERIFIED
hazards.detail: review_activity.ts:149-153 keys the ledger on `{reviewId: context.pr_id, chunkId: context.chunk.chunk_id, toolSchemaVersion}` + role+model+promptSha256 (invocation_ledger.ts::computeKey). pr_id is stable per-PR. promptSha256 is a deterministic transform of context. The load-bearing assumption is that `chunk.chunk_id` is DETERMINISTIC across a full re-run (re-clone→re-chunkAndRedact regenerates the same chunk_id for the same diff). If chunk_and_redact mints chunk_id with any randomness or path/order nondeterminism, the ledger MISSES on re-run and buys a second paid Bedrock completion — defeating gate #2. I could not confirm the chunk_id minting (Bash classifier outage); the orchestrator + ev_id comments assert 'deterministic per-chunk id'/'content-addressable UUIDv5', which is encouraging but must be PROVEN. Also: ledger is OPTIONAL on LlmClient — the shell MUST inject it (production review wiring does; platform jobs omit it).

hazards.title: Gate #1 (abort-aware side effects) — orchestrate() has NO abort-signal parameter; it only has the claimCheck callback at 4 boundaries
hazards.detail: orchestrate() takes `claimCheck?: () => Promise<void>` fired before clone, classify, aggregate, and (FIX #10) before post. Today that callback throws a non-retryable ApplicationFailure on lost lease. The shell's abort contract is DIFFERENT: runOneJob hands the handler an AbortSignal (work.signal) that fires on lease-loss OR the hard runtime ceiling, and the hard-timeout race can leave the handler ORPHANED still running (review_job_runner.ts:52-59 — markFailed fences late writes via attempt_token, but the in-flight orchestrate keeps executing). So an orphaned handler could still reach Step 9 post AFTER the job was failed/reclaimed. The fence protects core.review_jobs, but NOT GitHub posts or DB findings writes. Phase 2 must thread the AbortSignal into claimCheck (so it aborts BEFORE clone/classify/aggregate/post) AND ensure post/persist honor it — proving 'no external write after abort'. The 4 claim-check boundaries are necessary but the post boundary is the riskiest (FIX #10 is exactly the window-closure).

hazards.title: Gate #4 (reaper unification) — TWO liveness clocks exist today: the PR-mutex lease AND (new) the job lease
hazards.detail: The Temporal body's liveness is the per-PR mutex lease (renew_pr_review_mutex_lease + janitor sweep + the abortIfClaimLost claim-check). The Phase-1 runner adds a SECOND liveness clock: the job lease (review_jobs_repo.ts claim/heartbeat/leased_until/timeout_at + reapCrashLooped). ADR-0077's gate #4 demands ONE clock = the job lease. If the shell ALSO acquires the PR mutex (via the gate's startReviewForWebhook) the two leases can disagree: a job whose row lease is reaped (reapCrashLooped marks state='dead') while the PR mutex is still held leaks the mutex until ITS separate expiry. Plan must decide: either (a) drop the PR-mutex entirely and make the job row the sole concurrency+liveness primitive (preferred per gate #4), or (b) bind the mutex lifetime to the job lease. Note runOneJob.markFailed uses exponential backoff with jitter and a max_attempts→'dead' terminal — the shell's per-job crash-loop semantics replace Temporal's per-activity retry.

hazards.title: runLifecycleBookkeeping + advisory early-exit + arbitration gating must be replicated verbatim or delivery rows stick at PERSISTED
hazards.detail: After orchestrate returns, the body runs runLifecycleBookkeeping (workflow.ts:953) flipping core.review_findings to finalized/skipped/degraded from `state.postedReview` (the capture posting.ts::captureFromPostedReview writes). If the shell skips this, findings stay at delivery_outcome IS NULL forever. The H-2 dropped-state path (posting.ts::dispatchInlineSkippedLifecycle + post_review_results.activity.ts buildDroppedStateDetails) flips dropped rows on a post failure — the shell must thread `recordDeliverySkipped` into PostingLifecycleDeps. Also the path-filters-excluded-all EARLY EXIT (orchestrator 471-525) posts an advisory review and returns early — the shell inherits this for free since it lives inside orchestrate(), but bookkeeping after that early return path differs (no persisted findings). Arbitration (Step 7.7) is GATED on `ports.applyArbitration !== undefined && sa !== null`; if the shell omits the port, arbitration silently no-ops (footer absent).

maps.provingStrategy:
Discharge the four gates with targeted integration tests against a disposable Postgres (localhost:5433 throwaway, NEVER the cluster) driving the SHELL handler (not the Temporal workflow):

GATE #3 (post idempotency): a test that invokes the shell handler TWICE for the same job (same run_id/review_id/pr_id) against a stub GhReviewClient that records calls; assert `core.posted_reviews` has exactly ONE row (ON CONFLICT held), `createReview` called once, the second run took the lost-claim `updateReview` path, and the inherited publication_outcome matches. This is a direct extension of the existing post_review_results.activity integration test (it already drives doPost with a disposable PG + stub client — see DoPostDeps).

GATE #2 (LLM ledger replay): inject a real `LlmInvocationLedger` (or the in-memory `LlmInvocationLedgerPort` fake) + a counting stub LlmClient; run the shell handler, force a crash AFTER the first chunk's paid call but BEFORE markDone, re-run the job, and assert the stub Bedrock client was invoked ONCE per (pr_id, chunk_id) across both runs (the ledger.lookup HIT replays). Must FIRST add a unit test proving chunk_and_redact mints byte-identical chunk_ids for the same diff across two invocations (the load-bearing precondition).

GATE #1 (abort-aware side effects): run the shell handler with an AbortController that fires at the pre-aggregate claim-check boundary; assert NO postReview/persistReviewFindings/post GitHub call happened after the abort (the stub ports record zero calls past the abort point) and the handler rejected promptly. Reuse the runOneJob hard-timeout/lease-loss machinery to source the signal.

GATE #4 (reaper unification): a test that leases a job, lets the lease expire past timeout_at with attempts<max, asserts claim() reclaims it (mints attempts+1, new attempt_token) and the fence rejects the prior owner's markDone/markFailed (stale token → applied=false); plus a test that reapCrashLooped marks an expired+attempts-exhausted lease 'dead' — proving the JOB lease is the sole liveness clock with no second PR-mutex clock leaking. File targets: a new `apps/backend/test/integration/runner/review_job_shell.*.test.ts` alongside the existing runner + post_review_results integration tests.
openQuestions: WHERE does the shell load the full ReviewPullRequestPayloadV1 from? core.review_jobs carries only run_id/review_id/installation_id/delivery_id — the payload (gh_owner, head_sha, pr_number, changed paths, etc.) is NOT on the job row. Is it on the outbox/delivery row keyed by delivery_id, or a review_requests table? (Bash outage blocked confirming the producer/enqueue side.)

Is `chunk.chunk_id` from chunk_and_redact byte-stable across a full re-clone→re-chunk of the same diff? Gate #2's ledger key depends on it; orchestrator comments assert determinism but I could not read the chunk_id minting (classifier outage). MUST be proven before relying on the ledger.

ADR-0077 does not yet exist on this branch (docs/adr tops out at 0076); the task cites it as source of truth. Confirm its final number/location and whether it mandates KEEPING the PR-mutex+gate (startReviewForWebhook) or collapsing concurrency onto the job row alone (gate #4 reaper unification implies the latter).

Does the shell keep the Temporal GATE activity (startReviewForWebhook — tenancy recheck + mutex acquire) at all, or does the job-runner claim() subsume it? If kept, the two-lease leak hazard (gate #4) must be resolved.

How are the Temporal RETRY_POLICIES (per-activity at-least-once retry curves, e.g. bedrockReviewChunk 4 attempts, non-retryable Bedrock errors) re-expressed in-process when the whole job re-runs from scratch? Per-activity in-process retry, or rely solely on job-level re-run + the LLM ledger for cost safety?

Do degradation.ts's `metricMeter`/`inWorkflowContext` and posting.ts's `ActivityFailure`/`ApplicationFailure` behave correctly (no-op metrics, correct error-class detection) when imported in plain Node outside a workflow execution? Needs a smoke import test.

maps.subsystem: The Temporal review workflow body that wraps orchestrate() — apps/backend/src/workflows/review_pull_request.workflow.ts (reviewPullRequest), the control flow AROUND apps/backend/src/review/pipeline/orchestrator.ts (orchestrate)
maps.currentShape:
The review entrypoint is the Temporal workflow `reviewPullRequest(rawPayload: unknown): Promise<ReviewPullRequestResultV1>` in apps/backend/src/workflows/review_pull_request.workflow.ts. It is dispatched today via ingest→outbox→`temporal_workflow_start` sink (apps/backend/src/outbox/sinks/temporal_workflow_start.ts → `port.startWorkflow({workflowType:"reviewPullRequest", ...})`). The body does, in order:

1. PARSE: `ReviewPullRequestPayloadV1.parse(rawPayload)`.
2. GATE: `await startReviewForWebhook(rawPayload)` (proxyActivities → apps/backend/src/activities/start_review_for_webhook.activity.ts::startReviewForWebhook). The gate runs ONE DB transaction (withMutexTransaction) doing: tenancy re-check `SELECT enabled FROM core.repositories`, `acquirePrReviewMutex(...)` (apps/backend/src/concurrency/pr_mutex.ts), and an encrypted audit emit. A non-`accepted` status (skipped_busy/skipped_disabled/skipped_legacy_payload) SHORT-CIRCUITS — the body returns `pre` verbatim. On accepted it extracts `pre.mutex_id` (throws ApplicationFailure.nonRetryable `PrMutexMissingOnAccept` if null).
3. Builds two closures bound to mutexId: `claimStillHeld` → `renewPrReviewMutexLeaseActivity(mutexId)` (fail-OPEN on throw, returns false ONLY on definitive lost lease) and `abortIfClaimLost` → throws ApplicationFailure.nonRetryable `PrMutexLostClaim` when the lease is lost.
4. OUTER try/finally (FIX #1 hardening) opening the instant mutexId is in hand. Declares `workspaceHandle: WorkspaceHandle | null = null` and `const state = new ReviewWorkflowState()` hoisted to function scope.
   Inside the inner try: post_review_placeholder (best-effort stageOutcome), enrich_pr_files_v2 (REAL changed_paths/ranges; FIX #3 marks `pr_file_enrichment_failed` degradation on error), allocateWorkspace → assigns `workspaceHandle`, recordReviewLifecycleEvent(ANALYSIS_STARTED), fetch_linked_issues + fetch_suggested_reviewers (fail-open), fetch_manifest_snapshots→parse_manifest_dependencies (fail-open), load_parent_review_findings (carry-forward, flag-gated). It then builds `ReviewPipelineContext` threading `claimCheck: abortIfClaimLost`, `onPlaceholderTeardown` (delete_review_placeholder), `arbitrationNow: workflowInfo().startTime.toISOString()`, and calls `result = await orchestrate(ctx)`.
   After orchestrate: `runLifecycleBookkeeping(payload, state, result)` (flips core.review_findings delivery_outcome via recordDeliveryFinalized/Skipped/Degraded — bookkeeping-only, each try/caught), recordReviewLifecycleEvent(ANALYZED via buildAnalyzedPayload), finalizeReviewRun (RUNNING→COMPLETED).
   The `finally` runs `CancellationScope.nonCancellable(...)`: releasePrReviewMutexActivity(mutexId) ALWAYS, then releaseWorkspace IFF workspaceHandle!==null.
5. OUTER catch: `CancellationScope.nonCancellable` → isCancellation(exc) ? recordRunCancelled (RUNNING→CANCELLED) : recordRunFailed (RUNNING→FAILED), both best-effort/swallowed, then `throw exc`.
6. Maps `result`→`ReviewPullRequestResultV1` reading `state.postedReview.{reviewId,publicationOutcome}`.

orchestrate() itself fires `ctx.claimCheck()` at 4 boundaries (before clone, before classify, before aggregate, before post — the 4th is FIX #10 hardening) and has its own try/finally for workspace cleanup of the LEASE (the body release is the lifecycle backstop). The mutex/workspace lifecycle (signals, patched, cancellation scope) is ENTIRELY in the workflow body, not orchestrate().
keyFiles: apps/backend/src/activities/post_review_results.activity.ts:doPost (2-phase ON CONFLICT(pr_id) claim + markerFor + lost-claim update path)
maps.whatPhase2Changes:
The Phase-2 shell is a `JobHandler = (job: ReviewJobV1, signal: AbortSignal) => Promise<void>` (apps/backend/src/runner/review_job_runner.ts) that REPLICATES the reviewPullRequest body around an UNCHANGED orchestrate(). Concrete substitutions per Temporal mechanism:

- DISPATCH: the `temporal_workflow_start` outbox sink is replaced by `ReviewJobsRepo.enqueue({runId, reviewId, installationId, deliveryId})`. The shell reads the job's run_id/review_id/installation_id, then must reconstruct the full `ReviewPullRequestPayloadV1` (the job row does NOT carry it — see hazard). The runner's `runOneJob` already supplies claim/lease/fence/heartbeat/markDone/markFailed.

- proxyActivities → direct in-process calls. Every `proxyActivities<{...}>(opts)` (startReviewForWebhook, allocateWorkspace, releaseWorkspace, renewPrReviewMutexLeaseActivity, releasePrReviewMutexActivity, postReviewPlaceholder/delete, enrichPrFilesV2, fetch*, recordReviewLifecycleEvent, finalize/recordRun*, recordDelivery*) becomes a direct call to the activity function (these activities ALREADY run in normal Node and self-resolve their DSN/pool — they are not sandbox-bound). The orchestrate() activity ports (makeActivityPorts / build_activities) stay as-is.

- CancellationScope.nonCancellable(cleanup) → a plain try/finally whose finally body is NOT abort-interruptible (run the mutex+workspace release even after `signal` aborts). isCancellation(exc)/ApplicationFailure → the runner's existing catch (markFailed) plus an explicit abort branch.

- workflow.patched()/gate collapse → already collapsed-on straight-line code (COLLAPSED_GATES, zero Python histories); NOTHING to port — the shell inherits the straight-line body verbatim.

- workflowInfo().startTime.toISOString() (arbitrationNow, replay-safe instant) → must be replaced by a STABLE per-job timestamp the shell derives ONCE and persists/recomputes deterministically (e.g. job.started_at), since re-runs must reproduce the same `suppressed_at` value — there is no Temporal replay determinism anymore.

- ABORT THREADING (NEW, does not exist today): the shell must thread `signal` into the pipeline so that on lease-loss/abort NO external write happens after the abort point. Today neither orchestrate() nor the activities accept an AbortSignal (only a per-HTTP-call transportAbortSignal exists) — the abort-aware side-effect contract (gate 1) is the one genuinely new control-flow obligation.

- The four orchestrate() claimCheck boundaries continue to call `renewPrReviewMutexLease` (gate 4 lever); the runner's `heartbeat` lease is the SECOND liveness clock that must be unified.

hazards.title: The review-job shell has TWO liveness clocks; reaper-unification (gate 4) is NOT free
hazards.detail: Today liveness is the pr_review_mutex LEASE (core.pr_review_mutex.lease_expires_at, DEFAULT_LEASE_TTL_SECONDS=1800), renewed via renewPrReviewMutexLease at the 4 orchestrate() claimCheck boundaries; the reviewRunReaperWorkflow + mutexJanitorWorkflow sweep dead RUNNING rows. Phase 1 introduces a SEPARATE clock: core.review_jobs.{leased_until,timeout_at,heartbeat_at} driven by ReviewJobsRepo.heartbeat (review_job_runner.ts runOneJob hb loop). These are independent: a job lease can be live while the mutex lease expired (or vice-versa), letting the janitor reclaim the PR mutex and a superseding job start a 2nd concurrent paid review while the original job still holds its job lease. Unifying to ONE clock (the job lease) means either (a) the mutex acquire/renew/janitor stop being the liveness authority and the in-flight guard reads the job row, or (b) the shell renews BOTH in lockstep. The CLAUDE.md memory project_mutex_liveness_branch (ADR-0064) is the lease redesign this must not regress.

hazards.title: core.review_jobs does NOT carry the ReviewPullRequestPayloadV1 — the shell cannot reconstruct gate input
hazards.detail: ReviewJobsRepo.enqueue (review_jobs_repo.ts) and ReviewJobV1 (libs/contracts/src/review_jobs.v1.ts) persist only {job_id, run_id, review_id, installation_id, delivery_id, priority, attempts, max_attempts, state, lease fields}. But startReviewForWebhook AND the whole body need the FULL payload (head_sha, gh_owner, gh_repo_name, pr_number, repository_id, github_installation_id, pr_title/description, policy_revision, etc.). The Temporal path carried the payload as the workflow arg. Phase 2 must source the payload for the job — either add a payload JSONB column to core.review_jobs, or have the shell re-read it from a canonical row (core.pull_requests / the outbox payload). This is a prerequisite for ANY shell behavior and is currently unaddressed by Phase 1.

hazards.title: Abort-aware side-effect contract (gate 1) is structurally absent in the pipeline
hazards.detail: orchestrate() and every external-write activity (post_review_results.doPost, allocate/release workspace, recordReviewLifecycleEvent, persistReviewFindings, the GitHub posts) accept NO AbortSignal — grep found only fetch_linked_issues + api_client.ts transportAbortSignal (a per-HTTP-call timeout, unrelated). The only abort surface is the runner's work.signal (review_job_runner.ts). So 'do NO external writes after abort' cannot be honored today: once the shell aborts (lease lost / runtime ceiling), in-flight activities keep running to completion (post_review can still POST to GitHub, persist can still write). The current lease-loss defense is COOPERATIVE and check-point-based (abortIfClaimLost only fires AT the 4 claimCheck boundaries, between stages — never mid-activity). Phase 2 must either thread signal through orchestrate→activities, or rely on the post-side idempotency claim to neutralize post-abort writes (see post idempotency hazard). The hard runtime ceiling in runOneJob explicitly notes a handler may overrun 'orphaned — it violated the honor-signal contract' and relies on the fence.

hazards.title: Post idempotency (gate 3) is keyed on pr_id, NOT run_id — a re-run of the SAME PR correctly dedupes, but the marker-update path REWRITES the GitHub body every re-run
hazards.detail: post_review_results.doPost (post_review_results.activity.ts) claims via INSERT INTO core.posted_reviews (pr_id, marker) ON CONFLICT (pr_id) DO NOTHING. On a Phase-2 crash-and-rerun the SECOND run LOSES the claim, reads the existing github_review_id, and dispatches ghClient.updateReview (idempotent body refresh) inheriting the persisted publication_outcome — so it does NOT create a duplicate GitHub review. GOOD. BUT: (a) the AD-4 stale-write guard (assertCurrentRun) keys on run_id/review_id; a re-run mints a NEW run_id (review_job_runner re-claims; the job keeps the same run_id only if the shell reuses it) — if run_id changes across re-runs, assertCurrentRun may BLOCK the re-run's post as a stale write, stranding the review. (b) The lost-claim NULL-row in-flight window (IN_FLIGHT_WINDOW_SECONDS_DEFAULT=300) assumes a CONCURRENT winner, not a SEQUENTIAL re-run of the same crashed run — a re-run within 300s of a crash that double-422'd will raise PostReviewTransientError and retry-loop. Phase 2 must decide whether re-runs reuse run_id (so assertCurrentRun passes) and confirm the in-flight window semantics under sequential re-run.

hazards.title: LLM ledger (gate 2) dedupes paid calls across re-runs, but ONLY for chunk-review invocations — walkthrough/arbitration/fix-prompt/rerank LLM calls are NOT ledgered
hazards.detail: The ledger (invocation_ledger.ts) is wired (build_activities.ts: LlmClient ledger:LlmInvocationLedger.fromDsn(dsn)) and the chunk-review activity (review_activity.ts) passes idempotency:{reviewId:pr_id, chunkId:chunk.chunk_id, toolSchemaVersion}. The key (review_id+chunk_id+role+model+prompt_sha256+tool_schema_version) is fully deterministic across a whole-review re-run, so re-running replays stored chunk responses (no double Bedrock spend) — this is the gate-2 mechanism and it survives re-run, not just Temporal retry. HAZARD: invokeModel.idempotency is OPTIONAL; any call site that omits it (walkthrough generation, arbitration, fix-prompt theme, llm_backed_rerank) re-invokes Bedrock on every re-run. Phase 2's 're-run from scratch' multiplies the cost of every UN-ledgered LLM call. The plan must enumerate which LLM call sites pass idempotency and decide whether the un-ledgered ones are acceptable re-spend or need ledgering.

hazards.title: arbitrationNow uses workflowInfo().startTime (Temporal replay-determinism) — re-runs would write DIFFERENT suppressed_at
hazards.detail: review_pull_request.workflow.ts threads arbitrationNow: workflowInfo().startTime.toISOString() into ApplyArbitrationInput; it is written onto SUPPRESSED_BY_LLM decisions' suppressed_at. Under Temporal this is replay-stable. In the shell there is no replay clock; a naive `new Date()` (also clock-gate-banned in the old sandbox, but the shell is normal Node) would make each re-run write a different suppressed_at, and a re-run could double-apply or diverge arbitration state. Phase 2 must derive arbitrationNow from a stable per-job value (e.g. core.review_jobs.started_at) so re-runs are byte-identical.

hazards.title: runLifecycleBookkeeping + ANALYZED + finalize run AFTER post; a crash between post and finalize re-runs the whole pipeline
hazards.detail: The body posts to GitHub (orchestrate Step 9) THEN does runLifecycleBookkeeping → recordDeliveryFinalized/Skipped/Degraded → ANALYZED → finalizeReviewRun (RUNNING→COMPLETED). If the shell crashes after the GitHub post but before finalizeReviewRun, the re-run replays the ENTIRE pipeline (clone→…→post). Post is idempotent (pr_id claim → updateReview), and chunk LLM is ledgered, so this is bounded — but the lifecycle setters (recordDeliveryFinalized binds rfids→comment_ids from state.postedReview.commentIds) must be idempotent under re-run, and finalizeReviewRun has StateDrift non-retryable semantics that may reject a re-run if the run already advanced. Confirm these terminal transitions tolerate sequential re-run of the same run_id.

maps.provingStrategy:
Author an integration test (new file, e.g. apps/backend/test/integration/runner/review_job_shell_rerun.integration.test.ts) against a throwaway Postgres at localhost:5433 (NEVER the cluster — per MEMORY isolated-test-db rule) plus a stub GhReviewClient and a stub/in-memory LlmInvocationLedger + LlmClient. Scenario: enqueue ONE review job; run the shell handler to completion on attempt 1 with the stub LLM and stub GitHub recording every createReview/updateReview + every ledger store/lookup + every cost-cap charge. Then FORCE a crash (throw after orchestrate's post stage but before finalizeReviewRun, or abort the signal mid-pipeline), let runOneJob.markFailed re-ready the job, and run a SECOND attempt. Assert the four gates:
(1) abort: after signal.abort() mid-pipeline, assert zero NEW external writes occurred after the abort point (no extra ghClient.createReview, no extra persist) — proves the abort-aware contract.
(2) ledger: assert the LlmInvocationLedger.lookup returned a HIT for every chunk on attempt 2 and ghClient/Bedrock stub paid-call count did NOT increase (cost-cap stub charged once) — proves paid-call dedupe across re-run.
(3) post idempotency: assert exactly ONE row in core.posted_reviews for the pr_id, ghClient.createReview called once total and updateReview on the re-run (was_update path), and the final publication_outcome inherited correctly — proves no double-applied GitHub post.
(4) reaper unification: assert that for the duration of the job lease, the pr_review_mutex (or whichever single clock the plan chooses) cannot be reclaimed by a competing acquire while the job heartbeat is live, AND that on job-lease expiry the SAME sweep reclaims both — i.e. one liveness clock governs both review-run-stuck and mutex-stuck reclamation. Use the existing pr_mutex.ts acquire (returns busy on a live lease) as the assertion oracle.
openQuestions: Where does the shell get the full ReviewPullRequestPayloadV1? Add a payload JSONB column to core.review_jobs (migration), or re-hydrate from core.pull_requests/outbox by run_id? The gate (startReviewForWebhook) needs the raw payload.

Does a Phase-2 re-run REUSE the original run_id (so assertCurrentRun's AD-4 stale-write guard and finalizeReviewRun's StateDrift check pass) or mint a new one? This determines whether the post-review claim and run-lifecycle transitions survive re-run. The job row keeps a fixed run_id, so reuse seems implied — confirm against assertCurrentRun semantics (apps/backend/src/domain/stale_write_guard.ts).

Which liveness clock wins for gate-4 unification: retire the pr_review_mutex lease as the liveness authority and make the in-flight/janitor logic read core.review_jobs, OR keep the mutex and renew it in lockstep with the job heartbeat? The mutex still serves the per-PR busy/skip decision (skipped_busy) at the gate, so it likely cannot be fully removed — only its LEASE/liveness role unified.

Should the gate (startReviewForWebhook) even run inside the shell, or move to ENQUEUE time? Today it acquires the mutex synchronously; if enqueue already holds the PR slot, running it again in the handler on re-run could mis-classify as skipped_busy against the job's own prior lease.

How is the AbortSignal threaded through orchestrate()→activity ports without changing orchestrate() (ADR-0077 says orchestrate stays UNCHANGED)? Likely via a port-wrapper that pre-checks signal.aborted before each external-write activity, plus the post-side idempotency claim as the backstop for any write that races the abort.

Are the non-chunk LLM call sites (walkthrough, arbitration apply, fix-prompt theme, llm_backed_rerank) acceptable to re-spend on every re-run, or must they be ledgered too? Enumerate their invokeModel call sites and confirm idempotency context is passed (currently only review_activity.ts passes it).

maps.subsystem: In-flight LLM invocation ledger (ADR-0068) — the per-chunk paid-call dedupe/replay mechanism that makes coarse-grained whole-review re-run affordable.
maps.currentShape:
The ledger is a NARROW, content-addressable idempotency record, NOT a lease/owner/status table.

TABLE — `migrations/0003_llm_invocation_ledger.sql` creates `core.llm_invocation_ledger`: `idempotency_key text PRIMARY KEY`, `installation_id uuid NOT NULL`, forensic projection cols (`review_id uuid, chunk_id uuid, role text, model text, prompt_sha256 text, tool_schema_version text`), `provider_response jsonb NOT NULL`, `created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`. ONE index `llm_invocation_ledger_installation_id_idx`. There is NO status, NO owner, NO lease, NO attempt_token, NO TTL column. Registered in `libs/platform/src/db/tenant_scoped_tables.ts`.

REPO — `apps/backend/src/integrations/llm/invocation_ledger.ts::LlmInvocationLedger` (port `LlmInvocationLedgerPort`), injected a shared-pool `Kysely<unknown>` (ADR-0062; never owns/destroys the pool); `fromDsn(dsn)` routes through `tenantKysely`. Three methods:
- `computeKey(inputs: LlmInvocationKeyInputs): string` — pure deterministic `sha256hex(reviewId \0 chunkId \0 role \0 model \0 promptSha256 \0 toolSchemaVersion)` via `node:crypto.createHash` (NOT random; clock_random-gate sanctioned).
- `lookup({key, installationId}): Promise<Record|null>` — single `SELECT provider_response::text ... WHERE idempotency_key=$key AND installation_id=$iid::uuid` (PK + tenant filter, defense-in-depth; satisfies the raw-SQL tenancy gate token). Reparses JSON; a non-object row is treated as a MISS (returns null). NO held txn, NO FOR UPDATE — a plain point SELECT.
- `store({key, entry}): Promise<void>` — `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (idempotency_key) DO NOTHING` (content-addressable → racing retries are byte-identical no-ops). `provider_response` bound via `CAST($json AS jsonb)`.
Plus module fn `hashMessagesForLedger(messages)` = sha256hex of canonical `[{role,content}]` — the promptSha256 component, single-sourced with the client.

CHECK-THEN-RECORD at the paid edge — `apps/backend/src/integrations/llm/client.ts::LlmClient.invokeModel` (the ONLY consumer). The ledger is an OPTIONAL constructor arg (`ledger?: LlmInvocationLedgerPort`) AND requires an OPTIONAL per-call `idempotency?: {reviewId, chunkId, toolSchemaVersion}` context. When BOTH present (lines ~411-549): compute `idempotencyKey` from the deterministic inputs (promptSha256 via `hashMessages`), then `lookup` FIRST (before cost-cap + request archive). HIT (`isReplay=true`) → replay the stored `provider_response` and SKIP the paid SDK call, the cost-cap `checkOrRaise` reservation, the request-archive blob put, AND the post-call `recordCallCost` (so a re-run does NOT double-count `cost_daily`); telemetry.llm_calls + Langfuse STILL fire against the replayed response (replayable observability). MISS → cost-cap check (with lock-timeout-retry-once-then-fail-closed) → archive request → call SDK → on success `storeInvocation` the RAW response BEFORE returning (guarded: a ledger write failure is swallowed, degrading to pre-ADR-0068 re-pay, "strictly no worse"). When ledger OR idempotency absent → behaves exactly as frozen Python (invoke, no replay).

WIRED at — `apps/backend/src/worker/build_activities.ts:475-491` `ledgerClientFactory` threads `LlmInvocationLedger.fromDsn(dsn)` into the real role-keyed `LlmClientCache`. The idempotency CONTEXT is passed at exactly ONE call site: `apps/backend/src/review/review_activity.ts::doReview` (~line 149) for the `bedrock_review_chunk` per-chunk call — `{reviewId: context.pr_id, chunkId: context.chunk.chunk_id, toolSchemaVersion: REVIEW_TOOL_SCHEMA_VERSION}`.

KEY-STABILITY linchpin (proves replay across whole-review re-run): `chunkId = context.chunk.chunk_id` is `computeChunkId({path,start_line,end_line,body})` = UUIDv5(namespace, `path\nstart\nend\nsha256(body)`) in `libs/contracts/src/diff_chunking.v1.ts::computeChunkId` — deterministic across runs for unchanged diff content. `reviewId = context.pr_id` (`libs/contracts/src/review_context.v1.ts` `pr_id: z.string().uuid()`) flows from `payload.pr_id` → `pr.prMeta.pr_id`, the persistent PR identity (the `core.posted_reviews` PK), NOT the ephemeral run_id. `REVIEW_TOOL_SCHEMA_VERSION` (review_activity.ts:55) is a sha256 digest of the frozen tool schema literal — stable per process, invalidates the key when the tool schema (output shape) changes. So the full key is stable across a re-run with the same diff + model + tool schema.
keyFiles: apps/backend/src/integrations/llm/client.ts:LlmClient.invokeModel (check-then-record at ~411-549; storeInvocation; isReplay skip of cost-cap+archive+recordCallCost)
apps/backend/src/review/review_activity.ts:doReview (~149 the ONLY idempotency-context call site) + REVIEW_TOOL_SCHEMA_VERSION (:55)
libs/contracts/src/diff_chunking.v1.ts:computeChunkId (deterministic UUIDv5 chunk_id — the cross-run key stability linchpin)
apps/backend/src/worker/build_activities.ts:475-491 ledgerClientFactory (where the ledger is wired into LlmClientCache)

apps/backend/src/review/walkthrough_activity.ts:294 + apps/backend/src/analysis/curator.ts:254 + apps/backend/src/retrieval/llm_rerank.ts + apps/backend/src/review/fix_prompt/fix_prompt_theme_activity.ts:167 (UNLEDGERED paid invokeModel call sites — no idempotency context)
apps/backend/src/runner/review_job_runner.ts:JobHandler type + runOneJob (Phase-1 abort/lease machinery the shell plugs into)
maps.whatPhase2Changes:
The ledger mechanism itself needs NO code change for the per-chunk happy path — it already gives the core Phase-2 guarantee. The Phase-2 review-job shell (the concrete `JobHandler` in `apps/backend/src/runner/review_job_runner.ts::JobHandler`, replacing Phase-1's pluggable stub) calls `orchestrate()` UNCHANGED; on a hard crash the runner re-claims the lease and the shell re-runs the WHOLE review from scratch. The ledger is what makes that re-run affordable: the per-chunk Bedrock calls that already completed on the prior attempt REPLAY from `core.llm_invocation_ledger` (HIT → no second paid completion, no double cost_daily) because the key `(pr_id, chunk_id, role, model, promptSha256, toolSchemaVersion)` is identical across attempts.

What the shell must do that the Temporal workflow did: NOTHING extra is required around the ledger — the LlmClientCache is already ledger-wired in build_activities, and orchestrate() drives review_activity which already passes the idempotency context. The shell replicates the workflow's lifecycle (gate/mutex/workspace/placeholder/orchestrate/bookkeeping/finalizers per review_pull_request.workflow.ts) but the ledger is orthogonal to all of that.

The thing Phase-2 MUST decide about the ledger (plan line 68 describes a ledger with "status/owner/lease/attempt_token; poll-with-backoff; fenced takeover; lease TTL") — that richer protocol is NOT what migration 0003 built. The current ledger is record-after-success only; it does NOT mark a call as in-flight before the SDK call. So a CONCURRENT duplicate (two pods both claim-racing the SAME review before fence, or a crash mid-SDK-call) buys two paid completions for the same key — the dedupe only protects SEQUENTIAL re-runs where the first attempt's store committed. Phase-2 must either (a) accept that the job-lease fence + ON CONFLICT makes concurrent dupes rare-and-cost-bounded (the cheapest path; the ledger stays as-is), or (b) actually add the in-flight reservation columns the plan line 68 sketches. This is the central open design call for this subsystem.

hazards.title: Only the per-chunk call is ledgered — walkthrough, Tier-1 curator, rerank, and fix-prompt RE-PAY on every whole-review re-run
hazards.detail: The idempotency context is passed at exactly ONE call site: review_activity.ts::doReview (bedrock_review_chunk). The other four paid invokeModel call sites — walkthrough_activity.ts:294, analysis/curator.ts:254 (Tier-1 secondary-role synthesis), retrieval/llm_rerank.ts, and fix_prompt/fix_prompt_theme_activity.ts:167 — call invokeModel WITHOUT an idempotency context, so they are unledgered and buy a fresh paid Bedrock completion on each re-run. ADR-0077's affordability claim ('the LLM ledger replays paid calls') is TRUE only for the per-chunk fan-out, which is the bulk of spend but NOT all of it. Phase-2 must EITHER thread idempotency contexts into these four sites (each needs a stable reviewId+chunkId surrogate — walkthrough/curator are PR-level not chunk-level, so chunkId would have to be a stable synthetic like a purpose tag) OR explicitly accept the bounded re-pay and document it as a non-goal. This is the single biggest gap for proving gate (2).

hazards.title: Record-after-success only — no in-flight reservation; concurrent duplicate or mid-call crash double-pays
hazards.detail: The ledger stores the response only AFTER the SDK returns (client.ts storeInvocation, post-success). There is no 'reserve this key as in-flight' write before the SDK call. So if two workers run the SAME review concurrently (a fence gap, or the abandoned-handler-still-running scenario from plan line 73), or a crash lands between SDK-success and store, the key is not yet present and a second attempt re-pays. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING only protects the WRITE race (two stores), not the PAY race (two SDK calls). The plan line 68 'status/owner/lease/attempt_token; fenced takeover' protocol would close this but migration 0003 did not build those columns. The job-lease fence + SKIP LOCKED makes concurrent same-review execution rare, but the abort-aware HARD CONTRACT (plan line 73) explicitly warns the abandoned handler keeps running — that is exactly the window where an unfenced in-flight SDK call double-pays. Phase-2 must reconcile this against gate (4) reaper unification: the ledger has NO liveness clock of its own, which is actually ALIGNED with 'one liveness clock = the job lease' — but it means the ledger cannot detect/recover a stuck in-flight call.

hazards.title: Ledger-write failure is silently swallowed → silent re-pay on next attempt
hazards.detail: client.ts storeInvocation wraps ledger.store in try/catch and swallows (by design: 'a ledger write failure must not mask a successful invocation; a retry would re-pay, strictly no worse'). This is correct for liveness but means a persistent ledger-write outage (pool exhaustion, DSN drift, the JSONB-read-contract class of bug) degrades silently to full re-pay with NO counter/alert. There is no observability emit on a swallowed store failure. Phase-2 should add a bounded-cardinality counter (e.g. codemaster_llm_ledger_store_failed_total) so a re-pay storm is visible, otherwise the affordability guarantee can erode invisibly in production.

hazards.title: Unbounded growth — no retention/pruner on core.llm_invocation_ledger
hazards.detail: No DELETE/TTL/retention job exists for the table (grep found zero pruners; only the writer + tenant registration reference it). provider_response is full raw Bedrock JSON per chunk per review, grow-forever. The replay window only needs to cover a re-run after a crash (minutes), but rows live forever. Per CLAUDE.md tiered-storage discipline this is a cold-aggregate candidate. Phase-2 (or a Phase-0 follow-up) should define a retention window — e.g. prune rows older than the reconcile window (plan line 61: ~6min worst-case reviewChunk wall-time, or gated on lease-expiry). Bounded blast radius, but it is real unmanaged growth on the core data store.

hazards.title: Replay correctness is silently coupled to chunk_id + tool-schema determinism — a chunker or prompt-builder change invalidates the whole ledger
hazards.detail: The key includes promptSha256 (hash of the EXACT messages) and toolSchemaVersion (hash of REVIEW_TOOL_SCHEMA). This is the SAFE design — any change to the prompt builder (buildUserMessage/buildSystemPrompt) or tool schema changes the key, so a stale response is never replayed against a changed prompt. But it also means a re-run after a deploy that touched the prompt builder gets a 100% MISS rate and full re-pay (correctly, but the affordability assumption silently does not hold across that deploy boundary). Phase-2's proving strategy should assert the MISS-on-changed-prompt direction too, not just the HIT-on-identical direction, so the invalidation semantics are pinned. Also note pr_id/chunk_id are STRINGS here (uuid as text), which dodges the Temporal dict-UUID-key JSON-safety hazard — good — but the shell must ensure pr_id is the persistent PR id, not run_id, or every re-run MISSES.

maps.provingStrategy:
Extend the EXISTING `test/integration/llm/llm_invocation_ledger.integration.test.ts` (DB-gated via describeDb / CODEMASTER_PG_CORE_DSN against a DISPOSABLE Postgres — NEVER the cluster; migration 0003 applied) — it already proves the core HIT/MISS/no-context cases through two SEPARATE LlmClient instances (simulating a retry on a fresh worker). For Phase-2 gate (2) 'paid calls replay/dedupe on re-run', add these scenarios:

(A) WHOLE-REVIEW re-run dedupe at the activity layer, not just the client: drive review_activity.ts::doReview TWICE with the SAME ReviewContextV1 (same pr_id + same chunk.chunk_id + identical chunk body) against a counting SDK stub and a ledger-wired LlmClientCache; assert the SDK createMessage counter is 1 (not 2) across both runs, the cost-cap recordCallCost ran once (cost_daily not double-counted), and the structured findings are byte-identical. This proves the real call-site key derivation (REVIEW_TOOL_SCHEMA_VERSION + computeChunkId + pr_id) is stable across re-run, which the current client-level test does NOT exercise (it hand-passes {reviewId, chunkId, toolSchemaVersion: 'tsv-1'}).

(B) MISS-on-changed-prompt (invalidation direction): same pr_id+chunk_id but a different message body → assert a DIFFERENT key, a fresh SDK call, and a SECOND ledger row — pinning that a prompt/tool-schema change correctly bypasses replay.

(C) The UNLEDGERED-call-sites gap (gate 2 completeness): a test asserting walkthrough_activity / curator / rerank / fix_prompt re-pay on re-run (the SDK counter is 2 across two runs) — either as a RED test that drives Phase-2 to thread idempotency into them, or as a documented xfail pinning the accepted-re-pay decision.

(D) Concurrent-dup window (gate 2 + gate 4 boundary): two doReview calls racing the SAME key with a store-delay injected between SDK-success and store on the first → assert the current behavior (both pay) so the in-flight-reservation gap is pinned and the Phase-2 decision (accept vs add reservation columns) is made against a concrete failing/passing assertion, not prose.

The runner-layer abort gate (1) and post-idempotency gate (3) are proved in the shell handler tests (apps/backend/src/runner/...), not here; this subsystem's responsibility is strictly the paid-call replay/dedupe proof in scenarios A–D.
openQuestions: Will Phase-2 thread idempotency contexts into the four currently-unledgered paid call sites (walkthrough, Tier-1 curator, rerank, fix-prompt), or formally accept bounded re-pay for them? If threaded, what is the stable chunkId surrogate for PR-level (non-chunk) calls like walkthrough/curator — a fixed purpose tag? a content hash?

Does Phase-2 add the in-flight reservation protocol the plan line 68 sketches (status/owner/lease/attempt_token, poll-with-backoff, fenced takeover) to core.llm_invocation_ledger, or does it rely on the job-lease fence + ON CONFLICT and accept that a concurrent/mid-call-crash window can double-pay one completion? Note migration 0003 built NONE of those columns — adopting line 68 is a NEW migration (Pattern D against an active writer).

What retention window prunes core.llm_invocation_ledger? The replay window only needs to cover a post-crash re-run (minutes/the reconcile window per plan line 61), but rows currently live forever with full raw provider_response JSON — needs a pruner + tiered-storage classification.

Should a swallowed ledger-store failure (client.ts storeInvocation catch) emit a bounded-cardinality counter so a re-pay storm from a ledger-write outage is observable, rather than degrading silently to full re-pay?

Phase-0 cost-journal (plan line 59) derives call_id = the ADR-0068 idempotency_key — does the journal's reserve/settle/release need the ledger to also record a 'reserved' state, coupling the two and pushing toward the line-68 status columns? i.e. is the cost-journal what forces the in-flight protocol regardless of the dedupe question?

maps.subsystem: GitHub posting + post-review idempotency (the post-review-idempotency gate)
maps.currentShape:
The post stage is a 3-layer stack:

1. SANDBOX ORCHESTRATION (runs inside the Temporal V8 workflow isolate) — `apps/backend/src/review/pipeline/posting.ts::postReviewResults(ports, state, walkthrough, aggregated, pr, deps)`. Renders the walkthrough markdown (`renderWalkthroughForPost`), dispatches the `post_review_results` activity via the typed port (`ports.postReview`), and on success calls `captureFromPostedReview(state, posted, pr)` which writes `state.postedReview = {reviewId, commentIds, postedReviewPrId=pr.prMeta.pr_id, keptFindingIndices, publicationOutcome, degradationNotes, droppedClassifications}`. On the H-2 failure path it extracts dropped-state from the ApplicationFailure (`extractDroppedStateFromPostFailure`) and dispatches `record_delivery_skipped` inline best-effort, then re-raises. Also fires fail-open `update_pr_description` (GET-modify-PATCH the PR body) + `generate_fix_prompt` (advisory PR comment) AFTER the review lands.

2. THE DURABLE-MUTATION ACTIVITY (normal Node runtime, real DB+GitHub I/O) — `apps/backend/src/activities/post_review_results.activity.ts::doPost(input: PostReviewInputV1, deps: DoPostDeps): Promise<PostedReviewV1>`. The Sprint-14.D 2-phase atomic-claim flow keyed on `core.posted_reviews.pr_id` (PK):
   - Phase 1 (claim): ONE tx — `assertCurrentRun` AD-4 stale-write guard inside a SAVEPOINT, then `INSERT INTO core.posted_reviews (pr_id, marker) ... ON CONFLICT (pr_id) DO NOTHING RETURNING pr_id`. Non-empty RETURNING ⇒ won the claim. The INSERT omits github_review_id+publication_outcome → relies on column DEFAULTs (NULL / 'degraded_unposted'), which satisfy the IFF CHECK as a placeholder.
   - WON → `attemptCreateWithBodyOnlyFallback` (POST /pulls/{n}/reviews with inline comments → on GitHub 422 retry body-only → on double-422 return `{created:null}` = DEGRADED_UNPOSTED without raising). On inline success it does the comment_ids length-mismatch invariant check (`created.commentIds.length === keptFindings.length` else raise). Then Phase 2: `UPDATE core.posted_reviews SET github_review_id=…, publication_outcome=… WHERE pr_id=…`.
   - LOST → `SELECT github_review_id, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now()-posted_at)) AS age_seconds, publication_outcome FROM core.posted_reviews WHERE pr_id=…`. NON-NULL review_id ⇒ dispatch `ghClient.updateReview` (PUT /pulls/{n}/reviews/{id}, idempotent body refresh) and INHERIT the persisted publication_outcome. NULL review_id ⇒ within IN_FLIGHT_WINDOW (300s) raise `PostReviewTransientError` (Temporal retries); past window ⇒ return DEGRADED_UNPOSTED without mutating the row.

3. THE GITHUB CLIENT — `apps/backend/src/integrations/github/review_client.ts::GitHubApiReviewClient`. `createReview` = POST /reviews (event hard-coded 'COMMENT') THEN, only when comments non-empty, a follow-up GET /reviews/{id}/comments to learn per-comment ids in submission order → `CreatedReviewV1 {reviewId, commentIds}`. `updateReview` = PUT (NOT PATCH) /reviews/{id} body {body}. `findExistingReviewByMarker` = GET /reviews page-1 marker scan (complement, not the dedupe primary). `markerFor(prId)` = `<!-- codemaster:review-marker:${prId} -->` embedded in the body via `buildReviewBody`.

PERSISTENCE: `core.posted_reviews` (migrations/0001_baseline.sql:1684) PK=pr_id, cols github_review_id bigint NULL, marker, posted_at, updated_at, publication_outcome text DEFAULT 'degraded_unposted' NOT NULL. Two CHECKs: `ck_posted_reviews_outcome_review_id_iff` (degraded ⇔ github_review_id IS NULL) and the 3-value enum CHECK. comment_ids are NOT stored on posted_reviews — they are persisted onto `core.review_findings.github_comment_id` by `recordDeliveryFinalized` (review_findings_repo.ts:503) via `UPDATE ... FROM unnest(rfids, comment_ids) ... WHERE delivery_outcome IS NULL`. The `delivery_outcome` enum (0001:56) = {inline_delivered, body_only_fallback, failed, not_applicable}; NULL = not-yet-finalized. The workflow body's `runLifecycleBookkeeping` (review_pull_request.workflow.ts:953) reads `state.postedReview` to dispatch the three flips (finalized/skipped/degraded), all gated by `CODEMASTER_LIFECYCLE_WRITES_ENABLED` (default FALSE, dormant) in record_delivery_lifecycle.activity.ts.

The current Temporal liveness clock around orchestrate() is the per-PR mutex lease: `claimStillHeld()` → `renewPrReviewMutexLeaseActivity(mutexId)`, `abortIfClaimLost()` raises `PrMutexLostClaim` non-retryably (workflow.ts:394-412). This is a SEPARATE clock from the post-review atomic claim; orchestrate's three claim-check boundaries fire `ctx.claimCheck = abortIfClaimLost`.
maps.whatPhase2Changes:
The review-job shell handler `(job: ReviewJobV1, signal: AbortSignal) => Promise<void>` must replicate what the Temporal workflow body does AROUND orchestrate() — but the post-review subsystem is ALREADY built to be re-run-idempotent at GitHub, so the shell mostly inherits it. Concretely:

POST-REVIEW IDEMPOTENCY (gate 3) is satisfied STRUCTURALLY and survives a whole-review re-run with NO shell change to doPost, BECAUSE the atomic claim is keyed on `core.posted_reviews.pr_id` (the persistent PR identity, NOT the ephemeral run_id/job_id). On a crash-and-rerun: the second run's doPost LOSES the ON CONFLICT claim (row already exists), reads the persisted github_review_id, and takes the `updateReview` PUT path = an idempotent body refresh, NOT a second POST. No double-comment. The shell must therefore call `doPost` with the SAME pr_id, head_sha, walkthrough_md, aggregated as the first run (deterministic inputs) so the body PUT is a no-op-equivalent refresh. The shell does NOT need a Temporal patched gate (all 25 collapse per the greenfield port).

WHAT THE SHELL MUST DO that the runner does not yet do:
1. Thread an abort-aware boundary: the shell must NOT call doPost (or any GitHub write) after `signal.aborted` (lease lost / runtime ceiling). doPost itself is NOT abort-aware — it has no signal param. The shell must gate the POST behind a final abort check and, more importantly, treat the lease as the claim-check seam that the Temporal workflow currently implements via `abortIfClaimLost`/`renewPrReviewMutexLeaseActivity`. The Temporal mutex-lease clock disappears; the SHELL's job lease (review_jobs.leased_until/timeout_at/attempt_token) becomes the single clock.
2. Replicate `runLifecycleBookkeeping`: after orchestrate returns, read `state.postedReview` and dispatch the three delivery-lifecycle flips (finalized/skipped/degraded) — these are already idempotent via `WHERE delivery_outcome IS NULL`. The shell must construct `state` (ReviewWorkflowState) and thread the `claimCheck`/`onPlaceholderTeardown` callbacks onto the ReviewPipelineContext exactly as the workflow body does (lines 707-784), substituting lease-renewal for mutex-renewal.
3. The shell owns the placeholder (post_review_placeholder — already idempotent via marker scan) and the non-cancellable cleanup (mutex/workspace release → in the de-Temporal world, lease release + workspace release).

hazards.title: comment_ids are NOT persisted on core.posted_reviews — they live ONLY on the in-memory PostedReviewV1/state.postedReview capture; a re-run takes the updateReview PUT path which returns NO comment_ids, so the second run cannot finalize findings inline
hazards.detail: On the WON-claim path doPost gets comment_ids from the createReview follow-up GET and returns them on PostedReviewV1; the workflow body's runLifecycleBookkeeping uses them to set core.review_findings.github_comment_id. But core.posted_reviews stores ONLY github_review_id + publication_outcome — NOT the per-comment ids. On a whole-review RE-RUN, doPost LOSES the claim and takes the lost-claim updateReview branch (post_review_results.activity.ts:1175-1218), which returns `comment_ids` DEFAULT [] (the PostedReviewV1.parse at line 1208 omits comment_ids → []) and `was_update:true`. So if run #1 crashed AFTER posting to GitHub but BEFORE runLifecycleBookkeeping persisted github_comment_id (e.g. because CODEMASTER_LIFECYCLE_WRITES_ENABLED was off, or the flip activity hadn't fired), run #2 can NEVER recover the inline comment_ids — the findings stay at delivery_outcome IS NULL forever with no path to inline_delivered. The Phase-2 in-flight-ledger / idempotency design MUST either (a) persist comment_ids on a durable store (e.g. add to core.posted_reviews or a ledger row) so the re-run can re-finalize, or (b) re-fetch them via GET /reviews/{id}/comments on the lost-claim path. Today neither happens. This is the single biggest post-review-idempotency hole for the re-run-from-scratch model.

hazards.title: DEGRADED_UNPOSTED in-flight window (300s) is calibrated to the Temporal activity start_to_close_timeout, NOT the job lease — under the de-Temporal runner the disambiguation between 'winner in-flight' vs 'terminal-degraded' can misfire
hazards.detail: The lost-claim NULL-review_id disambiguation (post_review_results.activity.ts:1139-1167) uses IN_FLIGHT_WINDOW_SECONDS_DEFAULT=300, documented as 'MUST be >= the activity start_to_close_timeout'. In Phase 1 the runner has its OWN clocks: leaseS, heartbeatS, and maxRuntimeS (the hard runtime ceiling in runOneJob). If a crashed run #1 left a posted_reviews row with github_review_id NULL (mid-Phase-1-to-Phase-2 window or a double-422 terminal-degraded) and run #2 re-runs, the 300s window must be >= the runner's job lease/timeout for the in-flight-vs-terminal classification to be correct. With the SAME job re-claimed serially (not concurrently) the in-flight case shouldn't arise, but a lease-steal (runner_metrics records lease_steal when attempts>1) means TWO runners can be racing the same review concurrently — exactly the concurrent-POST scenario the atomic claim defends against. The Phase-2 plan must reconcile IN_FLIGHT_WINDOW with the job lease duration (the reaper-unification gate) — one clock, not two.

hazards.title: doPost has NO AbortSignal — it cannot honor the abort-aware side-effect contract; a lease-loss mid-POST still writes to GitHub and to core.posted_reviews
hazards.detail: The Phase-1 handler contract is `(job, signal) => Promise<void>` and runOneJob aborts `work.signal` on lease-loss or runtime-ceiling. But doPost (and attemptCreateWithBodyOnlyFallback, the Phase-1/Phase-2 DB transactions, and ghClient.createReview/updateReview) take no signal and check none. Once orchestrate reaches the post stage, a concurrent lease-steal cannot stop the POST or the github_review_id UPDATE. The abort-aware contract (gate 1: 'no external writes after abort') is currently UNENFORCEABLE inside the post stage. Mitigations the plan must choose between: (a) the atomic claim itself is the guard — a lease-lost run that re-enters doPost will lose the ON CONFLICT claim and take the idempotent PUT path, so a double-POST is structurally prevented EVEN without signal threading; (b) thread signal into doPost and bail before the POST if aborted. Note the existing Temporal design relies on (a) via assertCurrentRun (the AD-4 stale-write guard) inside Phase-1 — a superseded run_id loses the claim. The shell must preserve assertCurrentRun semantics: it RAISES StaleWriteError if run_id is not the current run, which is the real cross-run safety net, NOT the AbortSignal.

hazards.title: Lifecycle delivery flips are DORMANT by default (CODEMASTER_LIFECYCLE_WRITES_ENABLED defaults false) — github_comment_id is never persisted in the default config, so the re-run recovery path described above is doubly broken in the shipped default
hazards.detail: record_delivery_lifecycle.activity.ts::lifecycleWritesEnabled() reads CODEMASTER_LIFECYCLE_WRITES_ENABLED, default 'false'; when false, recordDeliveryFinalized/Skipped/Degraded return [] without touching the DB. So in the default dormant config, core.review_findings.delivery_outcome and github_comment_id stay NULL after EVERY review — the 'finalized' state never lands. This means the `WHERE delivery_outcome IS NULL` idempotency guard is moot (everything is always NULL) AND there is no persisted record that a review was delivered. The Phase-2 plan must decide whether the de-Temporal shell flips this on (making delivery_outcome the durable 'already delivered' marker that a re-run reads to skip re-work) or continues to rely solely on core.posted_reviews.github_review_id as the idempotency key. If it stays dormant, posted_reviews.github_review_id (+ the updateReview PUT) is the ONLY idempotency mechanism and the per-finding lifecycle is purely advisory.

hazards.title: update_pr_description (PATCH PR body) and generate_fix_prompt (POST advisory comment) run AFTER the post and are NOT idempotent across a whole-review re-run
hazards.detail: posting.ts::postReviewResults fires two more GitHub side-effects after captureFromPostedReview: `ports.updatePrDescriptionSummary` (GET-modify-PATCH the PR description, lines 328-345) and `ports.generateFixPrompt` (persists core.fix_prompts + posts a collapsed advisory PR comment, lines 357-383). Both are fail-open (stageOutcome swallows). On a re-run these fire AGAIN: the PR-description appendage may double-append the summary (depends on the activity's own GET-modify dedupe, not shown here), and generate_fix_prompt may post a second advisory comment unless the fix_prompts persistence or comment-post is marker-deduped. The plan's post-review-idempotency proof must extend beyond the review POST to these two trailing writes — they are the most likely 'double-applied on re-run' regression because they do NOT go through the posted_reviews atomic claim.

hazards.title: posting.ts runs inside the Temporal V8 sandbox and reads @temporalio/common ActivityFailure/ApplicationFailure to classify the H-2 dropped-state failure — the de-Temporal shell loses Temporal's exception-wrapping, so extractDroppedStateFromPostFailure will silently no-op
hazards.detail: extractDroppedStateFromPostFailure (posting.ts:162) narrows the error to `ApplicationFailure` OR `ActivityFailure(cause=ApplicationFailure)` and requires `type === POST_REVIEW_FAILED_WITH_DROPPED_STATE`. doPost RAISES exactly this via `ApplicationFailure.create({type: POST_REVIEW_FAILED_WITH_DROPPED_STATE, details:[buildDroppedStateDetails(...)]})`. In the Temporal world, the activity boundary wraps the ApplicationFailure in an ActivityFailure; posting.ts (running in the workflow) unwraps it. In the de-Temporal shell, if doPost is called IN-PROCESS (no activity boundary), the ApplicationFailure surfaces directly (the `err instanceof ApplicationFailure` branch still works) — so the unwrap is fine PROVIDED doPost still throws an `@temporalio/common` ApplicationFailure. If the plan replaces ApplicationFailure with a plain Error, the dropped-state skip dispatch silently breaks and classifier-dropped findings stay stuck at delivery_outcome NULL. The dependency on @temporalio/common as an error-carrier is a hidden coupling the shell must preserve or replace deliberately.

hazards.title: Two independent liveness clocks today (Temporal mutex lease via renewPrReviewMutexLeaseActivity AND the post-review IN_FLIGHT_WINDOW) — the reaper-unification gate must collapse BOTH onto the job lease without breaking the atomic-claim disambiguation
hazards.detail: The Temporal workflow currently has: (1) the per-PR mutex lease (claimStillHeld/abortIfClaimLost, workflow.ts:394-412) renewed at orchestrate's three claim-check boundaries; (2) the posted_reviews IN_FLIGHT_WINDOW (300s) for NULL-row disambiguation; (3) Temporal's own activity start_to_close_timeout/heartbeat. Phase 1 added a FOURTH: the job lease (leased_until/timeout_at/heartbeat_at/attempt_token in review_jobs_repo.ts). The reaper-unification gate (gate 4: ONE liveness clock = the job lease) requires the shell to DROP the mutex-lease activity (the job lease subsumes the per-PR mutex — but note: the PR mutex prevents two DIFFERENT reviews of the same PR from racing, while the job lease prevents two runners taking the same JOB; these are NOT the same scope — the plan must confirm the job-enqueue dedupe by (run_id|review_id) covers the per-PR supersession the mutex provided, or the PR-mutex semantics are lost). assertCurrentRun (the AD-4 stale-write guard) is the run-level supersession guard that must be preserved regardless.

maps.provingStrategy:
A DB-integration test driving doPost twice over a disposable Postgres (localhost:5433, never the cluster) with a scripted GhReviewClient stub — this is the natural extension of the existing post_review_results integration test pattern (the activity's own docstring at post_review_results.activity.ts:55-57 describes 'the integration test can drive it with a disposable PG + a stub client').

Concrete scenario discharging the post-review-idempotency gate (gate 3):
1. Seed core.review_findings with N persisted findings + a pr_meta. Run doPost(input, {ghClient: stubA, dsn}) where stubA.createReview returns {reviewId: 999, commentIds: [...N]}. Assert: ON CONFLICT claim WON, GitHub POST /reviews called exactly ONCE, core.posted_reviews row has github_review_id=999 + publication_outcome='inline_posted', PostedReviewV1.was_update===false, comment_ids length === N.
2. Re-run doPost(SAME input, {ghClient: stubB, dsn}) simulating a whole-review crash-and-rerun (same pr_id, head_sha, walkthrough_md). Assert: claim LOST, stubB.createReview called ZERO times, stubB.updateReview (PUT) called exactly ONCE with reviewId=999, returned PostedReviewV1.was_update===true, publication_outcome INHERITED='inline_posted' (read from the row, not hardcoded), core.posted_reviews still has exactly ONE row for pr_id (no duplicate). This proves no double-POST / no double-comment.
3. The comment_ids-loss hazard (the critical finding): assert that the re-run's PostedReviewV1.comment_ids === [] (current behavior) — this is the RED test that documents the gap; the Phase-2 fix (persist or re-fetch comment_ids) turns it green by asserting the re-run recovers the N comment_ids and can still finalize core.review_findings.github_comment_id.
4. Abort-aware (gate 1): a test where the AbortSignal is aborted BEFORE doPost is invoked must assert ZERO GitHub writes AND ZERO core.posted_reviews INSERT — discharged at the SHELL level (the handler must check signal before the post stage), not inside doPost.
5. Reaper-unification (gate 4): a chaos test (mirror the existing runner chaos test 'lease-steal fenced, completes once, loser=lease_lost' at commit 1129707) where two runners claim the same review_jobs row; assert exactly ONE wins the posted_reviews atomic claim (the fence at attempt_token + the assertCurrentRun guard), the loser gets lease_lost, and GitHub sees exactly one POST.

The existing chaos test for runOneJob (apps/backend/src/runner/*.test.ts, the '1129707 test(runner): chaos' commit) is the template for the concurrency proof; the post_review_results integration test (the doPost-with-disposable-PG harness referenced in the activity docstring) is the template for the single-runner idempotency proof.
openQuestions: ADR-0077 (docs/adr/0077-temporal-removal-postgres-job-runner.md) is NOT present on this branch worktree — the highest existing ADR is 0074/0066. The task states ADR-0077 is the source of truth 'also on this branch', but the file does not exist at /Users/ascoe/Projects/.cmb-worktrees/de-temporal-runner-phase1/docs/adr/. The planner must locate the real ADR (it may be on a different branch or not yet committed) before treating 'orchestrate() reused UNCHANGED inside the shell' as a verified contract.

Does the de-Temporal shell flip CODEMASTER_LIFECYCLE_WRITES_ENABLED on? If it stays dormant (default false), core.review_findings.delivery_outcome/github_comment_id are NEVER persisted, so posted_reviews.github_review_id is the SOLE idempotency key and the per-finding lifecycle is advisory-only. This changes whether the comment_ids-loss hazard is even reachable.

How should the comment_ids be made durable for re-run recovery? Options: (a) add a comment_ids column/JSONB to core.posted_reviews and write it in Phase 2; (b) re-fetch via GET /reviews/{id}/comments on the lost-claim path before returning PostedReviewV1; (c) accept that a crash between POST and finalize permanently strands inline-delivery state. The current code does (c) by omission.

Does the job-enqueue dedupe (by run_id|review_id|delivery_id in ReviewJobsRepo.enqueue) preserve the per-PR supersession semantics that the Temporal per-PR mutex (renewPrReviewMutexLeaseActivity) provided? The mutex prevents two DIFFERENT reviews of the same PR racing; the job lease prevents two runners taking the same JOB. If a newer review of the same PR enqueues a second job, what prevents both from posting? (assertCurrentRun guards run-level supersession at the posted_reviews claim, but only if both jobs share the same pr_id and the newer run_id is recorded as current.)

Is doPost expected to keep throwing @temporalio/common ApplicationFailure for the H-2 dropped-state path, or will the de-Temporal shell replace it? extractDroppedStateFromPostFailure in posting.ts depends on the ApplicationFailure type+details shape; replacing it silently breaks the classifier-dropped-findings skip dispatch.

Are update_pr_description (PATCH PR body) and generate_fix_prompt (advisory comment) individually idempotent across a re-run, or do they double-append/double-comment? Their own activity implementations (updatePrDescriptionSummary, generateFixPrompt) were not read in this pass and need a dedupe audit before the post-review-idempotency gate can claim full coverage.

maps.subsystem: Fix-prompt feature (generate + persist + post the copy-pasteable Claude-Code "fix-it" prompt) — re-run safety under the Phase-2 review-job shell
maps.currentShape:
The fix-prompt runs as the LAST stage of the post path, INSIDE orchestrate(), so the Phase-2 shell inherits it for free by calling orchestrate() unchanged. Concretely:

DISPATCH SITE — `apps/backend/src/review/pipeline/posting.ts::postReviewResults` (the `fix_prompt` stageOutcome block, ~lines 357-383): runs AFTER post_review + update_pr_description, UNCONDITIONAL when `aggregated.findings.length > 0 && ports.generateFixPrompt !== undefined`. Wrapped in `stageOutcome("fix_prompt", …)` with `handle.skipOutcome()` + explicit `recordStage({stage:"fix_prompt", outcome: fixPromptStageOutcome(...)})`; raiseAfterLog defaults false so any throw is swallowed (advisory — never fails the already-posted review). `postReviewResults` is reached from `orchestrator.ts::orchestrate` Step 9 via `runPostStage(ctx, walkthrough, aggregated, postingDeps)` (and the advisory path-filters-excluded-all Step 2a.1, which carries zero findings so the fix-prompt never fires there).

ACTIVITY — `apps/backend/src/activities/generate_fix_prompt.activity.ts::FixPromptActivities.generateFixPrompt(payload: GenerateFixPromptInputV1): Promise<FixPromptActivityResultV1>` (bound arrow property). Body: (1) empty findings → short-circuit not-generated; (2) `buildFixPrompt(...)` — makes a PAID LLM call (sonnet via modelForPurpose("fix_prompt")) for the best-effort `## Cross-cutting patterns` themes, fail-open to the deterministic base; (3) `await this.repo.persist(record, {installationId})`; (4) `recordFixPromptGenerated(...)`; (5) render comment then best-effort `this.gh.createIssueComment(...)` guarded only by `if (github_installation_id !== null)` inside a bare try/catch.

BUILDER — `apps/backend/src/review/fix_prompt/fix_prompt_theme_activity.ts::buildFixPrompt` (the paid LLM call: `client.invokeModel({purpose:"fix_prompt", installationId, ...})`) + `renderFixPromptComment` (pure).

PERSIST — `apps/backend/src/domain/repos/fix_prompt_repo.ts::FixPromptRepo.persist` is `INSERT INTO core.fix_prompts (...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (review_id) DO UPDATE SET ...`. Table `core.fix_prompts` PK = `review_id` (migrations/0001_baseline.sql:3652, `fix_prompts_pkey PRIMARY KEY (review_id)`). So the DB ROW is already idempotent/keyed on review_id (proven by test/integration/domain/repos/fix_prompt_repo.integration.test.ts "upserts idempotently … no dup row").

POST — `apps/backend/src/integrations/github/review_client.ts::GitHubApiReviewClient.createIssueComment` (line 269) is a BLIND `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{prNumber}/comments {body}` — NO marker, NO ON-CONFLICT, NO existing-comment lookup. Wired via `worker/build_activities.ts::makeLazyFixPromptIssueClient` (per-call installationId routing, deferred-Vault).

CONTRAST — the post-review path DOES have GitHub-post idempotency: `activities/post_review_results.activity.ts` claims `core.posted_reviews` via `INSERT … ON CONFLICT (pr_id) DO NOTHING` + an embedded hidden marker `<!-- codemaster:review-marker:{pr_id} -->` (`markerFor`, line 108) + winner/loser disambiguation. The fix-prompt has the DB upsert but NONE of the GitHub-post claim machinery.
keyFiles: apps/backend/src/activities/post_review_results.activity.ts:markerFor (L108) + posted_reviews ON CONFLICT claim (L966-972) — the idempotency PATTERN the fix-prompt lacks
maps.whatPhase2Changes:
The shell does NOT re-implement fix-prompt dispatch — it calls orchestrate() unchanged, and the fix-prompt rides inside posting.ts as today. What the shell must change is to make the fix-prompt's TWO external side effects re-run-safe, because on a crash the shell re-runs the WHOLE review from scratch (no Temporal replay, no per-activity dedup) — so generateFixPrompt CAN execute a 2nd time against the same review_id.

(1) PAID LLM CALL (buildFixPrompt → invokeModel purpose=fix_prompt): on re-run this re-bills unless the Phase-2 in-flight LLM ledger dedupes it. Phase-2 ledger keys on the ADR-0068 `idempotency_key`/`call_id`; the fix-prompt invokeModel must mint a STABLE, content-addressable idempotency_key (derived from review_id + the included-findings set) so a 2nd attempt replays the cached completion instead of re-paying. This is the SAME ledger gate the reviewChunk/walkthrough paid calls need; the fix-prompt is just one more paid consumer that must route through it. Today buildFixPrompt has no idempotency_key on the invokeModel call.

(2) GITHUB ISSUE-COMMENT POST (createIssueComment): the DB row is already idempotent (ON CONFLICT (review_id)), but the GitHub comment is a blind POST — a re-run posts a SECOND advisory "Fix-it prompt for Claude Code" comment to the same PR. The shell must make this re-run-safe via the plan's L70 design: a DB-fenced claim keyed on review_id that gates the POST. Two viable shapes: (a) the lightest — embed a hidden marker `<!-- codemaster:fix-prompt-marker:{review_id} -->` in renderFixPromptComment and have the activity call the EXISTING `listIssueComments` (already on the client, L288) to skip if a marker-bearing comment exists; or (b) mirror post_review_results — a `core.fix_prompts.comment_posted_at` / `github_comment_id` column updated under an ON-CONFLICT-fenced UPDATE so the POST is claimed exactly once (the `posted_reviews` pattern, adapted to the review_id PK that already exists). The persist already returns nothing about post-state; Phase-2 must thread the post-claim through the repo. The plan's own checklist (L70) names "the fix-prompt marker keyed on review_id + a DB-fenced claim (ON CONFLICT(review_id))" as the required Phase-2 deliverable.

(3) ABORT-AWARENESS: today neither buildFixPrompt nor createIssueComment receives the AbortSignal. The Phase-2 shell threads `signal` from runOneJob through orchestrate into the post path; the fix-prompt activity must (a) thread signal into the invokeModel + the GitHub fetch, and (b) re-check `signal.aborted` immediately BEFORE createIssueComment and bail rather than post — so a hard-timed-out, orphaned handler does not post a fix-prompt comment after the lease was already stolen by another pod.

hazards.title: GitHub fix-prompt comment is a blind POST — re-run double-posts the advisory comment
hazards.detail: GitHubApiReviewClient.createIssueComment (review_client.ts:269) does POST /issues/{prNumber}/comments with NO marker, NO existing-comment lookup, NO ON-CONFLICT.
